key_highlights: Global bond selloff deepens, with US 30-year yields hitting 2007 highs, driven by rising oil prices and hawkish Fed expectations.
Wall Street prices out rate cuts and eyes potential hikes, as inflation fears and geopolitical tensions escalate.
Risk-off sentiment dominates: stocks post worst day since March, Bitcoin ETFs see record outflows, and commodities slump.
Geopolitical risks surge: Trump's China trip yields limited progress on Iran/Hormuz, oil rises, and bond vigilantes return.
AI and tech remain bright spots: Cerebras IPO soars 90%, Roblox beats expectations, and AI buildout fuels Asian market gains.
Consumer strain evident: affordability crunch, rising gas prices, and inflation concerns weigh on retail and housing.
Central bank divergence: Fed faces division, UK leadership drama weakens pound, and Japan warns on yen spikes.

sectors.trend: Mixed: AI-related names (Cerebras, Roblox, Sea Limited) show strong performance, but chipmakers and broader tech sell off on rising yields.
sectors.implications: AI buildout remains a powerful long-term theme, but near-term volatility from macro headwinds and valuation concerns. Focus on companies with strong fundamentals and AI exposure.

sectors.trend: Bullish: Oil prices rise on geopolitical tensions (Iran/Hormuz) and supply constraints; gas expansion projects advance.
sectors.implications: Energy sector benefits from supply disruptions and inflation hedging. Oil pipeline projects (UAE, Alberta) and gas expansions (NRG) signal long-term demand. Watch for demand destruction from high prices.

sectors.trend: Severe selloff: Global bond yields spike, 30-year US yields at 2007 highs, credit markets under pressure.
sectors.implications: Rising yields reflect inflation fears and hawkish central banks. Bond vigilantes active. Investors shift to credit for yield, but spread widening risk looms. Duration risk is high.

sectors.trend: Weak: Affordability crunch, rising gas prices, and inflation weigh on spending. Retail sales lifted by tax refunds but drag from inflation looms.
sectors.implications: Consumer-facing companies face margin pressure and demand slowdown. Subscription fatigue and price sensitivity noted. Focus on value and essential goods.

sectors.trend: Positive: Post-quantum spending theme gains traction; Bank of Spain flags cyber risks.
sectors.implications: Cybersecurity remains a structural growth area, especially with AI and quantum threats. Companies like CHKP and others poised to benefit.

sectors.trend: Mixed: Mortgage rates little changed, but New York plans tax on cash home purchases over $1M. Zillow prepares for next revolution.
sectors.implications: Housing market faces affordability headwinds and regulatory changes. Commercial real estate under pressure from high rates. Renters insurance demand stable.

sectors.trend: Stable: Zoetis and GILD remain analyst favorites; Rani Therapeutics beats revenue estimates.
sectors.implications: Healthcare offers defensive characteristics amid market turmoil. Animal health and biotech have selective opportunities.

sectors.trend: Mixed: Copper and gold slump on inflation fears, but hedge funds boost bullish copper wagers. Lithium project advances.
sectors.implications: Base metals face demand uncertainty from global slowdown, but supply constraints and energy transition support long-term. Gold under pressure from rising yields.

sectors.trend: Volatile: Bitcoin ETFs see record outflows ($630M), but Bitcoin price holds at $82k as Clarity Act advances.
sectors.implications: Regulatory progress (Clarity Act) is positive, but macro headwinds and risk-off sentiment weigh. Kraken cuts workers, IPO delayed. Long-term adoption continues.

sectors.trend: Negative: Boeing deal with China falls short (200 vs 500 planes), Ford falls on AI rally abating, Honda retreats to hybrids.
sectors.implications: Auto and aerospace face demand and geopolitical challenges. EV transition hits speed bumps. Focus on hybrid and defense-related transport.

sectors.trend: Positive: NOC and other defense stocks benefit from geopolitical tensions and Iran conflict.
sectors.implications: Defense spending likely to increase amid global instability. US military losses in Iran war highlight need for modernization.

key_themes: Global bond selloff and rising yields
Geopolitical tensions: Iran/Hormuz, China trade, US politics
Inflation fears and hawkish central banks
Energy security and oil price spike
Regulatory shifts: crypto, real estate, AI
Central bank leadership changes and division

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration exposure, favor floating-rate instruments, increase allocation to short-term bonds or cash. Consider credit for yield but monitor spreads.
Increase exposure to energy and defense sectors. Hedge currency risk (USD strength). Diversify geographically, especially away from conflict zones.
Allocate to real assets (commodities, TIPS, real estate). Favor value stocks with pricing power. Reduce exposure to growth stocks with long-duration cash flows.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Central Bank Policy Error (Fed Hikes, Division)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Prepare for volatility. Use options for tail risk hedging. Monitor FOMC communications and leadership changes. Diversify across currencies.
Avoid overvalued consumer discretionary names. Focus on essentials, discount retailers, and services with recurring revenue. Monitor retail sales and credit card data.
Take profits on high-flying AI names. Diversify within tech (cybersecurity, enterprise software). Use stop-losses. Focus on companies with strong fundamentals and cash flows.
Reduce high-yield exposure. Favor investment-grade credit. Monitor corporate earnings and default rates. Consider private credit for yield but with liquidity premium.
Hedge foreign exchange exposure. Use options for yen and euro. Monitor central bank interventions. Diversify currency holdings.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory / Political Risk (Taxes, Crypto, Real Estate)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Stay informed on policy changes. Adjust real estate exposure (NY cash tax). Monitor crypto regulation (Clarity Act). Diversify across jurisdictions.
Increase energy and commodity exposure. Invest in domestic manufacturing and rare earth alternatives. Monitor oil prices and pipeline projects.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase allocation to energy and defense sectors
opportunities.rationale: Geopolitical tensions and supply disruptions support oil prices and defense spending. Energy companies with pipeline projects (UAE, Alberta) and defense contractors (NOC) are well-positioned.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy AI infrastructure and cybersecurity names on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: AI buildout is a multi-year trend. Cerebras IPO success and Roblox earnings confirm demand. Cybersecurity benefits from post-quantum spending. Use selloffs to accumulate.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in credit for yield, but focus on investment-grade and short duration
opportunities.rationale: With government bonds selling off, credit offers attractive yields. However, spread widening risk is present. Favor high-quality corporates and floating-rate notes.

opportunities.recommendation: Go long USD and short EUR/GBP
opportunities.rationale: Dollar strength driven by Fed hawkishness and safe-haven flows. Euro faces hedging flows but political risks in UK and eurozone weigh. Yen intervention risk but trend favors USD.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider commodities (copper, oil) for inflation hedge
opportunities.rationale: Supply constraints and energy transition support copper. Oil benefits from geopolitical risk. Hedge funds bullish on copper. Use ETFs for diversification.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce equity exposure and increase cash or short-term Treasuries
defensive_moves.rationale: Risk-off sentiment, bond selloff, and geopolitical risks warrant caution. Cash provides optionality to buy dips. Short-term Treasuries offer safety with decent yield.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge portfolio with put options on SPY or QQQ
defensive_moves.rationale: Market volatility likely to persist. Tail risk from geopolitical escalation or Fed error. Options provide asymmetric protection.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Rotate into defensive sectors (healthcare, utilities, consumer staples)
defensive_moves.rationale: Healthcare (ZTS, GILD) and utilities (DTE) offer stable earnings and dividends. Consumer staples provide inflation protection. Reduce cyclical exposure.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid long-duration bonds and high-yield credit
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising yields and spread widening risk make long-duration bonds unattractive. High-yield faces default risk in economic slowdown. Stick to short-duration investment grade.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and overvalued tech
defensive_moves.rationale: Consumer affordability crunch and rising rates hurt discretionary spending. Tech valuations are stretched. Take profits on high-flying names.

market_outlook.short_term: Bearish: Continued volatility from bond selloff, geopolitical tensions, and inflation fears. Stocks likely to test March lows. Safe havens (USD, gold, energy) may outperform. Rate cut expectations will be further pushed out.
market_outlook.long_term: Cautiously optimistic: AI and tech innovation remain powerful growth drivers. Energy transition and infrastructure spending provide structural support. However, inflation and geopolitical risks could persist. Diversification and quality are key.
key_catalysts: Fed FOMC meeting and dot plot (June 2026)
Iran/Hormuz negotiations and oil price trajectory
US-China trade deal progress (Boeing, Tesla, Nvidia)
Central bank leadership changes (Fed, UK, Japan)
AI and tech earnings season (Nvidia, Microsoft, OpenAI IPO)
US inflation data (CPI, PCE) and retail sales
Crypto regulation (Clarity Act) and Bitcoin ETF flows
Global bond auction results and yield levels
